CredentialRef.description: A credential the flow references by logical name; the vault (5.9) resolves it to a lazy handle at run time. No secret material is ever stored in flow data.

kind.description: Optional hint for the editor's credential picker (e.g. `http-basic`, `api-key`).

Edge.description: A wire from one node's output port to a downstream node. Branch = several edges from distinct ports of one node; merge = several edges into one node.

from-port.description: Source output port. Defaults to [`MAIN_PORT`]; [`ERROR_PORT`] is the error path; node-library node types may define others (e.g. a `conditional`'s `true`/`false`).

to-port.description: Target input port. Defaults to the node's single input; present only for future multi-input node types.

config.description: Opaque per-node configuration — a JSON object typed by the node library (5.3), not by this crate.

credential.description: Optional reference to a declared credential by [`CredentialRef::name`].

type.description: The node type — an open string the runner's node library (5.3) resolves (e.g. `postgres-query`, `transform`, `http-request`, `conditional`, `respond`, `delay`, `custom`). Structural validation here does not constrain it.

RowEvent.description: The row mutation a [`Trigger::RowEvent`] fires on.

Trigger.description: How a flow is invoked. The dispatcher (5.14) registers cron and row-event triggers; webhook triggers are routed by the API gateway.

oneOf.description: HTTP webhook. `sync` = respond within the request (write-ahead default, D15); otherwise fire-and-forget.

path.description: Optional path suffix under the flow's webhook route.

oneOf.description: Scheduled invocation (cron expression). Dispatcher-owned; wakes parked projects (F3).
Fires from a durable row event (outbox), e.g. F4 on `dispositions` insert — the outbox + doorbell path (D4, 5.14).

description: One version of a flow — the unit stored in the catalog and pointed at by the active-version pointer (deploying = flipping that pointer + a NATS doorbell, 5.14).

credentials.description: Credentials the flow needs, declared by logical name and resolved by the vault (5.9) at run time. Nodes reference these by [`Node::credential`]; secrets never appear in flow data.

edges.description: The wiring between node output ports and downstream nodes.

entry.description: The node the trigger payload enters the graph at.

flow-id.description: Stable identifier shared across every version of this flow.

trigger.description: How the flow is invoked. Exactly one.

title: Flow
